session_summary: An end-to-end witness session (2026-05-23): drafting and preregistering the W1/W2/W3 simulation hypotheses (todo/004), implementing and running them, and then scoping out the comprehensive sweep (todo/005/006) with a fresh-session handoff. Marcus drove preregistration discipline, repeatedly redirected engine and parallelization choices, probed numerical results (W2 Type I, finite-N residuals), supplied applied-data domain knowledge (NHANES/HRS missingness), pasted external Consensus search output, and made the scope and design calls (p=4, MAR vs MNAR, M cap, monotone missingness, R=2000).

coded_turns.quote: write todo/004-simulation-hypotheses.md as a pre-registered design document
coded_turns.note: Opening task: draft the preregistered W1/W2/W3 design doc with prediction/design/fallback, no code yet.

coded_turns.quote: Let’s preregister predictions
coded_turns.note: Directs that predictions be formally preregistered before running anything.

coded_turns.quote: You need to write these in a markdown and commit and push for them to be preregistered
coded_turns.note: Imposes the rule that preregistration means committed and pushed, not just drafted.

coded_turns.quote: Try with Amelia as imputation
coded_turns.note: Redirects the imputation engine to Amelia to test the W1 bias decomposition.

coded_turns.quote: get the fiml estimates and marginalize across Ymiss from the FIML estimate? I believe the derivation accommodates that
coded_turns.note: Proposes a FIML cross-check, judging that the derivation supports marginalizing over missing data.

coded_turns.quote: marginalize across Ymis analytically from the FIML estimate? I believe the derivation accommodates that
coded_turns.note: Refines prior turn: do the FIML marginalization analytically, not by imputation.

coded_turns.quote: I’m fine with just using Amelia and forgetting about norm imputation
coded_turns.note: Drops norm engine from scope; Amelia becomes primary, parking the norm anomaly.

coded_turns.quote: Good lord. Can we not just do an analytic hessian?
coded_turns.note: Pushes back on slow numeric Hessian; demands an analytic Hessian for speed.

coded_turns.quote: Can we parallelize across replications? Let’s to 20 codes
coded_turns.note: Directs parallelization across replications on 20 cores.

coded_turns.quote: Let’s move to w2
coded_turns.note: Closes W1 work and moves the session to W2.

coded_turns.quote: So read Chan carefully. I know they sat not to do a a*chi_sq+b like we did for mi-ic
coded_turns.note: Recalls that Chan declined the scaled-shifted form; directs a careful read of Chan.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: Chan (2022 AoS) MC reference vs SB scaled-shifted

coded_turns.quote: I swear we found good results with a satorra Bentler a*chi_swuare + b in MI-IC. Look it up
coded_turns.note: Supplies cross-project lead: Satorra-Bentler scaled-shifted worked in MI-IC; tells AI to check.

coded_turns.quote: What was delta?
coded_turns.note: Probes the local-alternative delta value behind the reported power numbers.

coded_turns.quote: Ok. Let’s test
coded_turns.note: Greenlights running the size-adjusted W2 test.

coded_turns.quote: We should probably do production to finalize conclusions of W2?
coded_turns.note: Pushes for a production-scale W2 run before drawing conclusions.

coded_turns.quote: Sorry are we talking advvantage of parallel processing
coded_turns.note: Checks that the W2 prod run will use parallelism before launching.

coded_turns.quote:
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Background command "Optimized W2 Amelia pilot
coded_turns.note: Background task-completion notification for the optimized W2 Amelia pilot (system-injected).

coded_turns.quote: Just run FIML. We’ll wait to do production over night
coded_turns.note: Defers the heavy Amelia prod run to overnight; run only fast FIML now.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: W2 FIML now, Amelia prod overnight

coded_turns.quote: I wonder if we did a SB correction to the difference in model deviances when making selection if that would work better
coded_turns.note: Proposes applying SB correction to the differential deviance in model selection (W3).
coded_turns.artifact_hint: SB MI-AIC arm on differential deviance (W3/W2 menu)

coded_turns.quote: Let’s go back to W2. Are we warm starting Lavaan?
coded_turns.note: Returns to W2 and asks whether lavaan fits are warm-started, flagging a likely overhead gap.

coded_turns.quote: Can’t you two loss functions and their gradients and do optim. One has the covariance as a parameter the other doesn’t
coded_turns.note: Proposes replacing lavaan overhead with two hand-coded loss+gradient optim fits (constrained/unconstrained).
coded_turns.artifact_hint: custom Cholesky optim replacing lavaan in chan_smi_test_k1

coded_turns.quote: Should we talk about how what we know now clarifies our potential— and I say potential because one MVN dies not make a skeptic convinced—contributions
coded_turns.note: Opens a framing discussion on contributions, cautioning one MVN example won't convince a skeptic.

coded_turns.quote: Do we need to give an example outside of the MVN data generation?
coded_turns.note: Questions whether a non-MVN example is needed to make the contribution credible.

coded_turns.quote: Yeah I agree with you and JAIGP seems to be pretty short articles
coded_turns.note: Agrees to stay MVN-scoped; supplies venue knowledge that JAIGP articles are short.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: scope decision: MVN-only, short JAIGP article

coded_turns.quote: What variables have large missingness and require auxiliary variables in public databases?
coded_turns.note: Reframes toward a real-data demonstration: which public-DB variables have high missingness needing auxiliaries.

coded_turns.quote: Can you look online and do some research about what the missingness is in NHANES lipid and what predicts it?
coded_turns.note: Directs online research on NHANES lipid-panel missingness and its predictors.

coded_turns.quote: Actually give me a consensus search query
coded_turns.note: Redirects from AI doing the search to AI handing him a Consensus query to run himself.

coded_turns.quote: Just give me the question that I can manually feed to consensus
coded_turns.note: Narrows the ask to a single manual-feed Consensus question.

coded_turns.quote: Sve this output from consensus verbatim that I will provide you
coded_turns.note: Announces he'll paste Consensus output and directs verbatim saving (provenance discipline).

coded_turns.quote: Auxiliary variables and missingness in NHANES lipids:** what is known
coded_turns.note: Pastes the full Consensus search result on NHANES lipid auxiliary variables/missingness.

coded_turns.quote: I asked this question to: What are the corresponding reported percent missingness of lipid variables in NHANES. Let me give you the results.
coded_turns.note: Announces a second Consensus query and that he'll paste its results.

coded_turns.quote: Reported missingness of lipid variables in NHANES
coded_turns.note: Pastes second Consensus result: only ~6-7% lipid missingness reported (Gao et al.).

coded_turns.quote: Exactly. But is NHANES longitudinal?
coded_turns.note: Catches that NHANES low missingness may be a poor fit; probes whether it is longitudinal.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: dataset choice: NHANES vs HRS (anisotropic missingness)

coded_turns.quote: Let me just do this query real quick in consensus
coded_turns.note: Signals he'll run another Consensus query himself.

coded_turns.quote: What are the corresponding reported percent missingness of variables in NHANES
coded_turns.note: States the broader NHANES percent-missingness query he is running.

coded_turns.quote: <summary>Background command "W2 Amelia production M=200 R=1000 — preregistered primary" completed
coded_turns.note: Background notification: preregistered primary W2 Amelia M=200 R=1000 production run finished.

coded_turns.quote: back to the consensus search: Save this verbatim:
coded_turns.note: Pastes third Consensus result on NHANES missingness and directs verbatim save.

coded_turns.quote: Let’s wait on the empirical demonstrations. What do you see as the contributions with the new results?
coded_turns.note: Defers the real-data demo; pivots to articulating the paper's contributions given new results.

coded_turns.quote: Remind me what C1, C2 etc refer to
coded_turns.note: Routine recall of the C1/C2 condition labels to ground the discussion.

coded_turns.quote: If C2 was .03 and C4 was .04 for the type 1 error, help me make sense of this statement the bias-corrected MI LRT controls Type I more precisely
coded_turns.note: Stress-tests the Type I claim against the actual numbers (.03 vs .04), forcing framing correction.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: W2 Type I framing (M-dependent calibration claim)

coded_turns.quote: But C5 SB is performing well in terms of Type 1 error?
coded_turns.note: Points out SB also controls Type I, challenging the bias-correction-necessity framing.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: W2 practitioner menu (SB as viable alternative)

coded_turns.quote: Agree with your analysis
coded_turns.note: Ratifies the revised M-dependent Type I / practitioner-menu framing.

coded_turns.quote: I think we update IDEAS.md for sure
coded_turns.note: Directs that the new W2 findings be written into IDEAS.md.

coded_turns.quote: We need to do more comprehensive simulation that includes different sample sizes, MCAR vs MAR vs MNAR. Other conditions?
coded_turns.note: Opens the comprehensive-sweep scope: vary N and missingness mechanism; asks for more conditions.

coded_turns.quote: How would we do uncongenial in a covariance structure model? Maybe we use a strong ridge prior on Amelia? I think we can stick to dimension 8.
coded_turns.note: Sets sweep design: MAR vs MNAR, ridge-prior uncongeniality, dim 8, M=20 vs 2000, FIML vs Amelia.
coded_turns.artifact_hint: todo/005 sweep design (uncongeniality via Amelia empri)

coded_turns.quote: Wouldn’t we want to do a much stronger empri like .5, just to exacerbate
coded_turns.note: Sharpens uncongeniality: use a stronger ridge prior (empri ~.5) to exaggerate the effect.

coded_turns.quote: M  should be Max 200
coded_turns.note: Caps the number of imputations at M=200 for the sweep, trimming the grid.

coded_turns.quote: We either need to use existing packages (I.e mice ampute functions for MAR) or have in code validation step that matches to existing where feasible
coded_turns.note: Sets validation standard (mice::ampute or match-to-existing checks); keeps p=4; proposes monotone missingness for max RIV-eigenvalue spread.

coded_turns.quote: I think we need 2000 replications across cells. Then let’s preregister our hypotheses, the. Write a todo list that I can take to a fresh session
coded_turns.note: Sets R=2000; directs preregistration then a fresh-session handoff todo list.

coded_turns.quote: Tell me about the hypotheses here
coded_turns.note: Asks the AI to lay out the sweep hypotheses for review before freezing.

coded_turns.quote: H3 can be expanded to include root N convergence—high missingness rate can be offset by higher N because additional information missing value provide matters less and less to the RIV as sample size increases
coded_turns.note: Supplies an asymptotic argument to expand H3 (rate-N tradeoff via RIV shrinking with N).

coded_turns.quote: Agree with option C
coded_turns.note: Ratifies Option C: a small separate 6-cell rate side-experiment (H3b) instead of expanding the main grid.

coded_turns.quote: Do we need to decide or recalibrate anything now that we are talking monotone missingness. And can we do this quickly here in this conversation.
coded_turns.note: Asks to settle monotone-missingness recalibration now; reiterates parallelism and warm starts.

coded_turns.quote: Agree do we want to do a sanity session as part of the plan with 100  replications
coded_turns.note: Agrees to the recalibration and proposes an R=100 sanity sweep as a wiring-bug check before production.

coded_turns.quote: Can you please give me the prompt for the next session
coded_turns.note: Requests the fresh-session resume prompt to hand off the sweep execution.

coded_turns.quote: And we’ve pushed?
coded_turns.note: Final check that the preregistration and todo files are committed and pushed.

ai_side.evidence: How would you like to drive this? A few possibilities: 1. Just sign off as drafted ... 2. Walk through predictions conversationally ... 3. ... "approved" plus today's date. Which fits?
ai_side.note: AI offered three ways to proceed on the preregistration before Marcus imposed commit-and-push.

ai_side.evidence: Now needs a decision on which reference distribution to implement next — (α) Chan-Meng F-reference (quicker) or (β) Chan (2022, AoS) MC reference (canonical preregistered). Which way do you want to go?
ai_side.note: AI surfaced the reference-distribution choice rather than picking silently.

ai_side.evidence: (α) Commit current state + run the FIML pilot at production scale ... (β) Investigate why norm::da.norm is off before committing ... (α) seems higher-priority
ai_side.note: AI laid out alpha/beta options with a recommendation on the norm anomaly.

ai_side.evidence: the calibration argument should be M-dependent, not a blanket claim ... Thanks for pushing on this — better to correct the framing now than have a reviewer catch it.
ai_side.note: AI flagged that the Type I claim was overstated and reviewer-vulnerable after Marcus's probe.

ai_side.evidence: The W2 reversal-of-reversal is interesting — the M=50 pilot misled us into thinking the prediction failed; at preregistered M=200 it holds. That's a useful lesson worth noting
ai_side.note: AI flagged that an underpowered pilot had misled the W2 conclusion, vindicating preregistration.

ai_side.evidence: Option C — Small separate rate experiment ... Option C is the most economical ... push back on my analysis above if you think W3-A actually should shrink with N
ai_side.note: AI proposed options for the rate experiment and invited Marcus to push back on its asymptotic reasoning.

ai_side.evidence: Confirm the grid and I'll start the p=8 implementation. Or push back on any dimension (drop N=1000? drop one missingness rate? hold M at 200 instead of M=20 vs 2000?).
ai_side.note: AI surfaced each open grid dimension for explicit sign-off before building.

proposed_codes.name: request-handoff
proposed_codes.definition: Asks the AI to package the current state into a resumable artifact (resume prompt, fresh-session todo, status doc) for a future session or person.
proposed_codes.example_quote: Write a todo list that I can take to a fresh session
